'''Sue no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (''sue no kuni no miyatsuko'') was a kuni no miyatsuko who ruled {{ill|Sue Province|ja|須恵国}} (the later {{ill|Sue District|ja|周淮郡}} of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}) along the present-day {{ill|Koito River|ja|小糸川|lt=Koito River basin|lt_ja=小糸川流域}} in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. Also written as '''Sue no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (周淮国造).

== Overview ==
*According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Ōfuhiomi-no-Mikoto|ja|大布日意弥命}}, son of {{ill|Takekoro-no-Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}} — ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}} — was appointed kuni no miyatsuko. He is said to have been appointed at the same time as his brother {{ill|Fukakaoomi-no-Mikoto|ja|深河意弥命}}, the first {{ill|Mukuda no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|馬来田国造}}.

=== Clan ===
'''{{ill|Hasetsukabe clan|ja|丈部氏}}''' (''hasetsukabe-uji''). Possibly of the same clan as the {{ill|Tsukuba no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|筑波国造}}, {{ill|Shinaga no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|師長国造}}, {{ill|Mukuda no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|馬来田国造}}, {{ill|Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}}, {{ill|Michinoku-Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造}}, {{ill|Michinokuchi-Kihe no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}}, {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, and {{ill|Suō no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|周防国造}}.

== Base ==

=== Territory ===
The territory of the kuni no miyatsuko was the region then called '''Sue Province''', corresponding to the later {{ill|Sue District|ja|周淮郡}} of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} — present-day {{ill|Kimitsu, Chiba|ja|君津市|lt=Kimitsu City|lt_ja=君津市}} and parts of {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市|lt=Kisarazu City|lt_ja=木更津市}} and {{ill|Futtsu, Chiba|ja|富津市|lt=Futtsu City|lt_ja=富津市}} in Chiba Prefecture. The {{ill|Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|ja|内裏塚古墳群}} in Futtsu City, beginning with the {{ill|Dairizuka Kofun|ja|内裏塚古墳}}, is said to be the burial place of the Sue chieftains or the kuni no miyatsuko clan, while the {{ill|Bentenyama Kofun|ja|弁天山古墳}} is considered the chieftain's tomb of the Sue no Kuni no Miyatsuko from around the end of the 5th century.

== Tutelary Shrine ==
Unknown.

=== Related Shrines ===
'''{{ill|Yakumo Shrine (Kimitsu, Miosuga)|ja|八雲神社 (君津市三直)|lt=Yakumo Shrine|lt_ja=八雲神社}}''' (''yakumo jinja'')
*: A shrine in {{ill|Kimitsu, Chiba|ja|君津市|lt=Kimitsu City|lt_ja=君津市}} in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. Though not a {{ill|Engishiki shrine|ja|式内社}}, it is said to have originated when, in the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, Ōfuhiomi-no-Mikoto enshrined {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命}} as the tutelary deity of Sue.

=== Tombs ===
*'''{{ill|Dōsojin-ura Kofun|ja|道祖神裏古墳}}''' (''dōsojin-ura kofun'')
*: A 56-m-long {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun (rectangle)|ja|前方後方墳|lt=zenpōkōhōfun|lt_ja=前方後方墳}} in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture, said to have been built in the first half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}.
*'''{{ill|Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|ja|内裏塚古墳群}}''' (''dairizuka kofungun'')
*: A kofun cluster in {{ill|Futtsu, Chiba|ja|富津市|lt=Futtsu City|lt_ja=富津市}} in Chiba Prefecture, made up of 11 {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}}, 7 {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}}, and 29 {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}}, built between the 5th and {{ill|7th centuries|ja|7世紀}}.

== Descendants ==

== See also ==
*{{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

== Footnotes ==
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist}}

== References ==
*''Nihon-shi Sōran'' ({{ill|Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}})

== External links ==
* [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/sue.html Walking the Kofun of Bōsō: Kofun of Sue Province]

{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:すえのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]]
[[Category:上総国]]
[[Category:千葉県の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:須恵国造]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
